title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.title: Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe | Authentic Indian

seo.imageAlt: Platter of vibrant red charcoal grilled tandoori chicken pieces with lemon wedges and cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky flavor and tender texture of our homemade Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, a true Indian delight!

description: Experience the fiery flavors and tender succulence of Tandoori Chicken, expertly charcoal grilled to perfection. This authentic Indian recipe brings the smoky essence of a traditional tandoor oven right to your backyard, promising a memorable dinner. Marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, each bite is a burst of aromatic delight.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry. Make deep gashes or scores on each piece, about 1-2 inches apart, to allow the marinade to penetrate deeply. This is crucial for flavor.

instructions.title: First Marinade Application
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 tablespoon of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub this mixture all over the chicken, ensuring it gets into the gashes. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes while you prepare the second marinade.

instructions.title: Second Marinade Preparation
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the plain yogurt, remaining ginger garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, Kashmiri red chili powder, mustard oil, and a pinch of food color if using. Mix until a smooth, thick paste forms.

instructions.title: Combine and Marinate
instructions.text: Drain any excess liquid from the chicken pieces. Add the second marinade mixture to the chicken, ensuring every piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: Arrange charcoal in your grill and light it. Let the charcoal burn until it turns ash-grey, indicating it is ready for cooking. Ensure you have a medium-hot fire. Lightly grease the grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the hot grill grates. Cook for about 25-35 minutes, turning occasionally to ensure even cooking and charring on all sides. Baste the chicken with any remaining marinade or a little oil during the last 10 minutes to keep it moist and enhance flavor. The chicken should be cooked through and have a beautiful charred exterior.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill. Let it rest for a few minutes. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with lemon wedges, onion rings, and a side of mint chutney or raita.

tips: Always use thick yogurt for the marinade to ensure it clings well to the chicken and prevents it from becoming watery during grilling.
For extra smoky flavor, you can place a piece of burning charcoal in a small heatproof bowl within the marinade, drizzle oil over it, and cover for a few minutes before grilling.
Do not overcrowd the grill. Cook in batches if necessary to maintain consistent heat and allow proper charring without steaming the chicken.
article:
Mastering the art of Indian cuisine at home often feels like a daunting task, but some dishes, while appearing complex, are surprisingly accessible and incredibly rewarding. Among them, Tandoori Chicken stands as a true icon, known for its vibrant red hue, smoky aroma, and succulent, spice-infused meat. While traditionally cooked in a scorching hot clay oven called a tandoor, achieving that authentic flavor and texture is absolutely possible in your backyard with a charcoal grill. Prepare to transform your next meal into an unforgettable feast with this guide to making the most incredible Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. This isn't just a recipe; it's an invitation to explore the rich, vibrant flavors of Indian dinner recipes right from your own kitchen.

## What is Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken?

Tandoori Chicken is a classic North Indian dish featuring chicken marinated in yogurt and a blend of aromatic spices, then traditionally cooked in a tandoor. The high heat of the tandoor gives the chicken a distinctive char, a smoky flavor, and keeps the meat incredibly moist and tender on the inside. When we talk about Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, we’re aiming to replicate that magical tandoor effect using the intense, radiant heat and smoky essence of a charcoal grill. The yogurt marinade acts as a tenderizer, while the spices (like ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, and chili) infuse every bite with a complex, savory, and slightly tangy taste. The charcoal grill adds an irreplaceable layer of smokiness and char that an oven simply cannot provide, making it the preferred method for achieving truly authentic results at home. It’s an Indian dinner recipe that promises a burst of flavor and a fantastic culinary experience.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken starts with selecting quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Chicken:
*   2-3 lbs chicken pieces (drumsticks, thighs, or a whole chicken cut into 8 pieces), skin removed and scored deeply with a knife (this helps the marinade penetrate).

For the First Marinade (optional, but highly recommended for deeper flavor):
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon salt
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice

For the Second Marinade:
*   1 cup full-fat plain yogurt (Greek yogurt or hung curd works best as it’s thicker)
*   2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste
*   2 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder (for color and mild heat; adjust to your spice preference)
*   1 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   1 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   A pinch of orange or red food coloring (optional, for the traditional vibrant color)
*   2 tablespoons mustard oil (optional, for pungent flavor) or any neutral oil

For Grilling and Serving:
*   Oil for brushing the grill grates and chicken
*   Lemon wedges, fresh coriander leaves, and red onion rings for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create succulent Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make 2-3 deep slashes on each piece of chicken. This helps the marinade penetrate the meat and cook evenly.
2.  **First Marinade (Optional but Recommended):** In a large bowl, combine the chicken with 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1 tablespoon lemon juice. Rub this mixture all over the chicken, ensuring it gets into the scores. Let it sit for 30 minutes at room temperature, or refrigerate for an hour.
3.  **Prepare the Second Marinade:** In another large bowl, whisk together the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, crushed fenugreek leaves, lemon juice, salt, and food coloring (if using). If using mustard oil, add it now. Mix until well combined and smooth.
4.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the first-marinated chicken to the second marinade. Ensure every piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or ideally overnight (up to 24 hours) for the best flavor penetration. The longer, the better!
5.  **Prepare Your Charcoal Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the coals for a two-zone fire: one side with direct high heat and the other with indirect medium heat. The ideal temperature should be around 375-400°F (190-200°C). Clean and oil your grill grates to prevent sticking.
6.  **Grill the Chicken:** Remove the chicken from the refrigerator about 30 minutes before grilling to bring it closer to room temperature. Shake off any excess marinade, but ensure the chicken is still well-coated. Place the chicken pieces over the direct high heat side of the grill.
7.  **Sear and Cook:** Sear the chicken for 2-3 minutes per side, until you see nice char marks. Then, move the chicken to the indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and continue to cook, turning occasionally, for about 20-30 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) at the thickest part. Brush lightly with oil a couple of times during the indirect cooking phase to keep it moist and enhance browning.
8.  **Final Char (Optional):** For an extra smoky flavor and crispier skin, move the chicken back over direct heat for the last 2-3 minutes, watching carefully to prevent burning.
9.  **Rest and Serve:** Transfer the Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken to a platter, tent loosely with foil, and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring tender, moist chicken.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home is all about attention to detail.

*   **Thick Yogurt is Key:** Use Greek yogurt or hang regular yogurt in a cheesecloth for an hour to drain excess water. This prevents the marinade from sliding off the chicken and ensures a thick, flavorful coating that adheres beautifully.
*   **Don't Skip the Scoring:** Deep scores in the chicken allow the marinade to penetrate deeply, infusing flavor into every fiber and helping the chicken cook more evenly.
*   **Marination Time Matters:** While 4-6 hours is acceptable, an overnight (12-24 hour) marination will yield the most flavorful and tender results for your Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken.
*   **Mind Your Heat:** A two-zone fire is crucial. Start with direct high heat for searing and charring, then move to indirect medium heat to cook through without burning the exterior. Don't overcrowd the grill; cook in batches if necessary.
*   **Basting for Moisture and Color:** Brushing the chicken with a little oil or melted butter during the indirect cooking phase helps keep it moist and contributes to that beautiful reddish-brown color.
*   **Add Wood Chips for Extra Smoke:** For an even deeper smoky flavor, soak a handful of wood chips (like apple or hickory) for 30 minutes, then add them directly to your hot coals during the grilling process.

## Serving Suggestions

Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a star in its own right, but it truly shines when paired with traditional accompaniments. Here are some serving suggestions to complete your Indian dinner recipes experience:

*   **Bread:** Warm naan bread, roti, or paratha are perfect for scooping up the flavorful chicken and any leftover juices.
*   **Chutney:** A refreshing mint-coriander chutney or a tangy tamarind chutney provides a delightful contrast to the rich chicken.
*   **Salad:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumbers, tomatoes, onions with lemon juice and spices) adds freshness and crunch.
*   **Rice:** Basmati rice or jeera rice (cumin-flavored rice) makes for a satisfying side, especially if you have extra marinade to drizzle.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based raita (cucumber or boondi raita) helps balance the spices.
*   **Onions and Lemon:** Thinly sliced red onions and plenty of lemon wedges for squeezing over the hot chicken are essential.

## Variations and Substitutions

While classic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is divine, there's always room for personalization:

*   **Different Cuts of Chicken:** Boneless chicken thighs or breasts can be used, but adjust cooking times accordingly (they will cook faster). For breasts, ensure not to overcook to avoid dryness.
*   **Vegetarian Tandoori:** The marinade works wonderfully for paneer (Indian cheese), mushrooms, cauliflower florets, or even bell peppers. Marinate for a shorter time (1-2 hours) and grill until tender and charred.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Kashmiri red chili powder to your preference. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper. For less, reduce the chili powder.
*   **Flavor Boosters:** A pinch of ajwain (carom seeds) in the marinade can add a unique aromatic depth.
*   **Oven or Air Fryer (If No Grill):** While the charcoal smoke is irreplaceable, you can bake Tandoori Chicken in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 25-35 minutes, finishing under the broiler for char. An air fryer at 375°F (190°C) for 15-20 minutes also works, but again, you'll miss the smoky notes that make Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken so special.

Embrace the smoky char and tender, spiced perfection of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. This Indian dinner recipe is a testament to the power of simple ingredients transformed by vibrant spices and the magic of the grill. Gather your ingredients, fire up your charcoal, and prepare to impress your taste buds with an authentic taste of India, right in your backyard.
